headline: Trump Reinstates the Iran Blockade and Demands a 20% Toll as America Becomes Hormuz’s “Guardian”
summary: Trump just claimed the right to charge every ship on Earth a 20% toll to pass through one of the world's most critical waterways, and oil markets are already reacting to what that could mean for energy prices, global trade, and U.S. alliances.

content.title: Trump Reinstates the Iran Blockade and Demands a 20% Toll as America Becomes Hormuz’s “Guardian”
content.summary: Trump just claimed the right to charge every ship on Earth a 20% toll to pass through one of the world's most critical waterways, and oil markets are already reacting to what that could mean for energy prices, global trade, and U.S. alliances.

headline: Chip toolmaker ASML expected to shine light on capacity and China challenges
summary: ASML, the biggest supplier of equipment used to manufacture AI chips, reports quarterly earnings on Wednesday looking to justify its chunky valuation and demonstrate how it will contend with U.S. moves to block ‌exports to China.  Clouding the outlook, however, is a proposed U.S. law requiring U.S. allies to align with export controls to curb China's ability to make advanced chips, with ASML named in the legislation.  The company has denied selling its most advanced EUV tools to China, which is forecast to account for up to 20% of ASML's sales this year through legal purchases of ‌less-advanced DUV tools to make chips for automotive, ⁠industrial and electronic products.

content.title: Chip toolmaker ASML expected to shine light on capacity and China challenges
content.summary: ASML, the biggest supplier of equipment used to manufacture AI chips, reports quarterly earnings on Wednesday looking to justify its chunky valuation and demonstrate how it will contend with U.S. moves to block ‌exports to China.  Clouding the outlook, however, is a proposed U.S. law requiring U.S. allies to align with export controls to curb China's ability to make advanced chips, with ASML named in the legislation.  The company has denied selling its most advanced EUV tools to China, which is forecast to account for up to 20% of ASML's sales this year through legal purchases of ‌less-advanced DUV tools to make chips for automotive, ⁠industrial and electronic products.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: An ASML flag and signage at the company's offices in Veldhoven

headline: AMD Rallies 5%, Intel Rises 4% as Cooling Inflation Sparks a Chip Rebound
summary: A cooler-than-expected inflation print sent chip stocks surging Tuesday, but AMD sitting at a 185x P/E after a 150% year-to-date run raises a sharp question: is this a breakout toward $600 or the setup for a painful unwind?

headline: ThoughtLab Collaborates with Urban Leaders to Provide Cities with an Evidence-Based AI Playbook and Benchmarking Model
summary: NEW YORK, July 14, 2026--Breakthroughs in AI offer cities extraordinary opportunities to improve economic competitiveness, social impact, and citizen services. But realizing AI’s full potential will only happen if urban leaders are able to overcome challenges around governance, data, resources, and public trust.

content.title: ThoughtLab Collaborates with Urban Leaders to Provide Cities with an Evidence-Based AI Playbook and Benchmarking Model
content.summary: NEW YORK, July 14, 2026--Breakthroughs in AI offer cities extraordinary opportunities to improve economic competitiveness, social impact, and citizen services. But realizing AI’s full potential will only happen if urban leaders are able to overcome challenges around governance, data, resources, and public trust.

headline: VLUE vs. VTV: Does the Value Factor Beat Just Buying Vanguard’s Value ETF?

headline: AMD Stock Jumps. Why This Analyst Sees 31% Upside.
summary: Advanced Micro Devices  got a big vote of confidence Tuesday as the artificial-intelligence trade was getting back in gear.  KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh, in a research note on the semiconductor sector, included a price target hike for AMD to $725 from $530.  The firm maintained its Overweight rating on the chip stock, with the analyst writing KeyBanc remains “positive” on the shares.

content.title: AMD Stock Jumps. Why This Analyst Sees 31% Upside.
content.summary: Advanced Micro Devices  got a big vote of confidence Tuesday as the artificial-intelligence trade was getting back in gear.  KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh, in a research note on the semiconductor sector, included a price target hike for AMD to $725 from $530.  The firm maintained its Overweight rating on the chip stock, with the analyst writing KeyBanc remains “positive” on the shares.
